stem: A quality control study surveys 20 manufactured units and observes 4 positive outcomes out of 20 (20.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (8 observations, rate = 12.5%) and Group B (12 observations, rate = 25.0%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 31% and 42% of the population. Estimate the true product defect rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.2. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.1848 (range [0.174, 0.1939]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.0807, 0.416]. Point estimate: 0.1848.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.36: 0.36×0.069 + 0.64×0.25 = 0.1848.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=20, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
